[[sn:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu)]][[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608848]] Wikidata name: Miwa-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miwa Shrine | native_name = 美和神社 | image = File:Miwa Shrine of Kiryu.JPG | caption = Worship Hall (Haiden) | map_type = Japan Gunma | map_caption = location in Gunma Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.417833|N|139.33975|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-1-1 Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | established = During the reign of Emperor Sujin | former_Rank = {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha (minor)|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft（小）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha (郷社)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | festivals = November 19–20 (Kiryu Ebisu Festival) | prefecture= {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社, *Miwa Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is listed as a minor {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. ==Overview== Miwa Shrine enshrines {{ill|2=ja|Ōmononushi Kushi-mika-tama-no-Mikoto|ja|大物主櫛甕玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} and {{ill|2=ja|Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|武速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. It is situated at the southeastern foot of Mount Agatsuma in Kiryu, facing Yamate-dori Avenue. The shrine is known for hosting performances of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] and taiko drumming during the annual {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} held from November 19–20. A mikoshi (portable shrine) storehouse for the {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} is located adjacent to the worship hall. ==History== Miwa Shrine is traditionally said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin, with its deity transferred from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. It was officially recognized as a shrine in 796 (Enryaku 15) alongside [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In 880 (Gangyō 4), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, alongside other shrines such as [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]], {{ill|2=ja|Kohachisu Shrine|ja|甲波宿禰社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and {{ill|2=ja|Oiwai Shrine|ja|小祝社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The shrine was referred to as "Conferred First Rank Miwa Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|2=ja|Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. Unfortunately, it lost many of its historical records and treasures in a fire during the Tenshō era. Miwa Shrine was originally affiliated with {{ill|2=ja|Enman-ji Temple|ja|円満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and both were managed by the Kojima family during the Edo period. It was designated as a Gōsha in 1872 (Meiji 5) and merged with the former [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] in 1908 (Meiji 41). ==Sub-Shrines== * '''Yasaka Shrine''' – Former Village shrine, merged in 1908 * '''Yagokoro-omoikane Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Hata Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Ichigami Shrine (Horinwa Gongen)''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Matsuo Shrine''' * '''Kotohira Shrine''' * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' ==Nearby Sites== * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu-ga-oka Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} ==References== * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 (Kiryu City History) |year=1958 |publisher=桐生市史刊行委員会 |editor=桐生市史編纂委員会}} * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 別巻 (Kiryu City History - Supplement) |year=1971 |publisher=桐生市役所 |editor=桐生市史別巻編集委員会}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/ Official Kiryu City Website] ==See Also== * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}} {{translated page|ja|美和神社 (桐生市)|version=101958614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) [[File:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]

In 1908, the [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Kiryu)]] of Honmachi 3-chome, previously dedicated to Gozu Tenno and attached to Shujōin Temple, was merged into Miwa Shrine. == Sub-Shrines (境内社) == * Yasaka Shrine – Previously a village shrine, merged in 1908. * Yaokotonashi Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Kikami Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Ichigami Shrine (Hōrinkan Gongen) – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Matsuo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} == Cultural Events == The shrine is the center of the {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}}, held annually from November 19 to 20. [[sn:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu)]][[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608848]] Wikidata name: Miwa-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miwa Shrine | native_name = 美和神社 | image = File:Miwa Shrine of Kiryu.JPG | caption = Worship Hall (Haiden) | map_type = Japan Gunma | map_caption = location in Gunma Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.417833|N|139.33975|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-1-1 Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | established = During the reign of Emperor Sujin | former_Rank = {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha (minor)|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft（小）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha (郷社)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | festivals = November 19–20 (Kiryu Ebisu Festival) | prefecture= {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社, *Miwa Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is listed as a minor {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. ==Overview== Miwa Shrine enshrines {{ill|2=ja|Ōmononushi Kushi-mika-tama-no-Mikoto|ja|大物主櫛甕玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} and {{ill|2=ja|Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|武速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. It is situated at the southeastern foot of Mount Agatsuma in Kiryu, facing Yamate-dori Avenue. The shrine is known for hosting performances of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] and taiko drumming during the annual {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} held from November 19–20. A mikoshi (portable shrine) storehouse for the {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} is located adjacent to the worship hall. ==History== Miwa Shrine is traditionally said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin, with its deity transferred from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. It was officially recognized as a shrine in 796 (Enryaku 15) alongside [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In 880 (Gangyō 4), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, alongside other shrines such as [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]], {{ill|2=ja|Kohachisu Shrine|ja|甲波宿禰社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and {{ill|2=ja|Oiwai Shrine|ja|小祝社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The shrine was referred to as "Conferred First Rank Miwa Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|2=ja|Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. Unfortunately, it lost many of its historical records and treasures in a fire during the Tenshō era. Miwa Shrine was originally affiliated with {{ill|2=ja|Enman-ji Temple|ja|円満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and both were managed by the Kojima family during the Edo period. It was designated as a Gōsha in 1872 (Meiji 5) and merged with the former [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] in 1908 (Meiji 41). ==Sub-Shrines== * '''Yasaka Shrine''' – Former Village shrine, merged in 1908 * '''Yagokoro-omoikane Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Hata Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Ichigami Shrine (Horinwa Gongen)''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Matsuo Shrine''' * '''Kotohira Shrine''' * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' ==Nearby Sites== * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu-ga-oka Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} ==References== * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 (Kiryu City History) |year=1958 |publisher=桐生市史刊行委員会 |editor=桐生市史編纂委員会}} * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 別巻 (Kiryu City History - Supplement) |year=1971 |publisher=桐生市役所 |editor=桐生市史別巻編集委員会}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/ Official Kiryu City Website] ==See Also== * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}} {{translated page|ja|美和神社 (桐生市)|version=101958614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) [[File:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall == Overview == '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is located in {{ill|Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|ja|宮本町 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|1=Miyamoto-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454887}}, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Ranked as a "small shrine," and was previously designated as a Gōsha (local shrine) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine venerates the deities [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (Ōmononushi Kushi Mikatama no Mikoto) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takesu Susanoo no Mikoto). == History == The shrine is traditionally believed to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Sujin, the 10th emperor of Japan. It is said to have been dedicated to the deities brought from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. In Enryaku 15 (796), the shrine was Ranked as an official shrine along with {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|ja|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634952}} (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]). During the Gangyō period (880), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Junior Grade, alongside other local shrines, as documented in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Sandai Jitsuroku]]. According to the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine was listed as "Miwa Daimyōjin of First Rank." Unfortunately, during the Tenshō era, a fire destroyed many of the shrine's old records and treasures. Historically, the temple {{ill|Enmanji (Kiryu)|ja|円満寺 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmanji (Kiryu)|1=Enman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394812}} served as its bettō-ji (attached temple). During the Edo period, the local Kojima family managed both the shrine and its associated temple. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was officially designated as a Gōsha (local shrine). In 1908, the [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Kiryu)]] of Honmachi 3-chome, previously dedicated to Gozu Tenno and attached to Shujōin Temple, was merged into Miwa Shrine. == Sub-Shrines (境内社) == * Yasaka Shrine – Previously a village shrine, merged in 1908. * Yaokotonashi Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Kikami Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Ichigami Shrine (Hōrinkan Gongen) – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Matsuo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} == Cultural Events == The shrine is the center of the {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}}, held annually from November 19 to 20. It features Kagura dances and taiko drum performances. Additionally, the shrine is involved in the {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}}, where its mikoshi (portable shrine) is stored in an adjacent warehouse. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Kiryu Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Park|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|Kiryu City North Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu City North Elementary School|1=Kiryu City North Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Worship Hall == Overview == '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is located in {{ill|Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|ja|宮本町 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|1=Miyamoto-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454887}}, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Ranked as a "small shrine," and was previously designated as a Gōsha (local shrine) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine venerates the deities [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (Ōmononushi Kushi Mikatama no Mikoto) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takesu Susanoo no Mikoto). == History == The shrine is traditionally believed to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Sujin, the 10th emperor of Japan. It is said to have been dedicated to the deities brought from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. In Enryaku 15 (796), the shrine was Ranked as an official shrine along with {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|ja|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634952}} (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]). During the Gangyō period (880), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Junior Grade, alongside other local shrines, as documented in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Sandai Jitsuroku]]. According to the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine was listed as "Miwa Daimyōjin of First Rank." Unfortunately, during the Tenshō era, a fire destroyed many of the shrine's old records and treasures. Historically, the temple {{ill|Enmanji (Kiryu)|ja|円満寺 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmanji (Kiryu)|1=Enman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394812}} served as its bettō-ji (attached temple). During the Edo period, the local Kojima family managed both the shrine and its associated temple. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was officially designated as a Gōsha (local shrine). In 1908, the [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Kiryu)]] of Honmachi 3-chome, previously dedicated to Gozu Tenno and attached to Shujōin Temple, was merged into Miwa Shrine. == Sub-Shrines (境内社) == * Yasaka Shrine – Previously a village shrine, merged in 1908. * Yaokotonashi Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Kikami Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Ichigami Shrine (Hōrinkan Gongen) – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Matsuo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} == Cultural Events == The shrine is the center of the {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}}, held annually from November 19 to 20. It features Kagura dances and taiko drum performances. Additionally, the shrine is involved in the {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}}, where its mikoshi (portable shrine) is stored in an adjacent warehouse. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Kiryu Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Park|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|Kiryu City North Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu City North Elementary School|1=Kiryu City North Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Kiryu)}} {{translated page|ja|美和神社 (桐生市)|version=101958614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[sn:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu)]][[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608848]] Wikidata name: Miwa-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miwa Shrine | native_name = 美和神社 | image = File:Miwa Shrine of Kiryu.JPG | caption = Worship Hall (Haiden) | map_type = Japan Gunma | map_caption = location in Gunma Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.417833|N|139.33975|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-1-1 Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | established = During the reign of Emperor Sujin | former_Rank = {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha (minor)|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft（小）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha (郷社)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | festivals = November 19–20 (Kiryu Ebisu Festival) | prefecture= {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社, *Miwa Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is listed as a minor {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. ==Overview== Miwa Shrine enshrines {{ill|2=ja|Ōmononushi Kushi-mika-tama-no-Mikoto|ja|大物主櫛甕玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} and {{ill|2=ja|Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|武速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. It is situated at the southeastern foot of Mount Agatsuma in Kiryu, facing Yamate-dori Avenue. The shrine is known for hosting performances of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] and taiko drumming during the annual {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} held from November 19–20. A mikoshi (portable shrine) storehouse for the {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} is located adjacent to the worship hall. ==History== Miwa Shrine is traditionally said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin, with its deity transferred from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. It was officially recognized as a shrine in 796 (Enryaku 15) alongside [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In 880 (Gangyō 4), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, alongside other shrines such as [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]], {{ill|2=ja|Kohachisu Shrine|ja|甲波宿禰社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and {{ill|2=ja|Oiwai Shrine|ja|小祝社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The shrine was referred to as "Conferred First Rank Miwa Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|2=ja|Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. Unfortunately, it lost many of its historical records and treasures in a fire during the Tenshō era. Miwa Shrine was originally affiliated with {{ill|2=ja|Enman-ji Temple|ja|円満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and both were managed by the Kojima family during the Edo period. It was designated as a Gōsha in 1872 (Meiji 5) and merged with the former [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] in 1908 (Meiji 41). ==Sub-Shrines== * '''Yasaka Shrine''' – Former Village shrine, merged in 1908 * '''Yagokoro-omoikane Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Hata Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Ichigami Shrine (Horinwa Gongen)''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Matsuo Shrine''' * '''Kotohira Shrine''' * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' ==Nearby Sites== * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu-ga-oka Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} ==References== * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 (Kiryu City History) |year=1958 |publisher=桐生市史刊行委員会 |editor=桐生市史編纂委員会}} * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 別巻 (Kiryu City History - Supplement) |year=1971 |publisher=桐生市役所 |editor=桐生市史別巻編集委員会}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/ Official Kiryu City Website] ==See Also== * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|美和神社 (桐生市)|version=101958614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) [[File:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall == Overview == '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is located in {{ill|Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|ja|宮本町 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|1=Miyamoto-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454887}}, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Ranked as a "small shrine," and was previously designated as a Gōsha (local shrine) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine venerates the deities [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (Ōmononushi Kushi Mikatama no Mikoto) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takesu Susanoo no Mikoto). == History == The shrine is traditionally believed to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Sujin, the 10th emperor of Japan. It is said to have been dedicated to the deities brought from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. In Enryaku 15 (796), the shrine was Ranked as an official shrine along with {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|ja|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634952}} (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]). During the Gangyō period (880), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Junior Grade, alongside other local shrines, as documented in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Sandai Jitsuroku]]. According to the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine was listed as "Miwa Daimyōjin of First Rank." Unfortunately, during the Tenshō era, a fire destroyed many of the shrine's old records and treasures. Historically, the temple {{ill|Enmanji (Kiryu)|ja|円満寺 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmanji (Kiryu)|1=Enman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394812}} served as its bettō-ji (attached temple). During the Edo period, the local Kojima family managed both the shrine and its associated temple. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was officially designated as a Gōsha (local shrine). In 1908, the [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Kiryu)]] of Honmachi 3-chome, previously dedicated to Gozu Tenno and attached to Shujōin Temple, was merged into Miwa Shrine. == Sub-Shrines (境内社) == * Yasaka Shrine – Previously a village shrine, merged in 1908. * Yaokotonashi Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Kikami Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Ichigami Shrine (Hōrinkan Gongen) – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Matsuo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} == Cultural Events == The shrine is the center of the {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}}, held annually from November 19 to 20. It features Kagura dances and taiko drum performances. Additionally, the shrine is involved in the {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}}, where its mikoshi (portable shrine) is stored in an adjacent warehouse. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Kiryu Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Park|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|Kiryu City North Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu City North Elementary School|1=Kiryu City North Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū [[sn:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu)]][[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608848]] Wikidata name: Miwa-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miwa Shrine | native_name = 美和神社 | image = File:Miwa Shrine of Kiryu.JPG | caption = Worship Hall (Haiden) | map_type = Japan Gunma | map_caption = location in Gunma Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.417833|N|139.33975|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-1-1 Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | established = During the reign of Emperor Sujin | former_Rank = {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha (minor)|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft（小）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha (郷社)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | festivals = November 19–20 (Kiryu Ebisu Festival) | prefecture= {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社, *Miwa Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is listed as a minor {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. ==Overview== Miwa Shrine enshrines {{ill|2=ja|Ōmononushi Kushi-mika-tama-no-Mikoto|ja|大物主櫛甕玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} and {{ill|2=ja|Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|武速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. It is situated at the southeastern foot of Mount Agatsuma in Kiryu, facing Yamate-dori Avenue. The shrine is known for hosting performances of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] and taiko drumming during the annual {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} held from November 19–20. A mikoshi (portable shrine) storehouse for the {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} is located adjacent to the worship hall. ==History== Miwa Shrine is traditionally said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin, with its deity transferred from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. It was officially recognized as a shrine in 796 (Enryaku 15) alongside [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In 880 (Gangyō 4), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, alongside other shrines such as [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]], {{ill|2=ja|Kohachisu Shrine|ja|甲波宿禰社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and {{ill|2=ja|Oiwai Shrine|ja|小祝社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The shrine was referred to as "Conferred First Rank Miwa Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|2=ja|Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. Unfortunately, it lost many of its historical records and treasures in a fire during the Tenshō era. Miwa Shrine was originally affiliated with {{ill|2=ja|Enman-ji Temple|ja|円満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and both were managed by the Kojima family during the Edo period. It was designated as a Gōsha in 1872 (Meiji 5) and merged with the former [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] in 1908 (Meiji 41). ==Sub-Shrines== * '''Yasaka Shrine''' – Former Village shrine, merged in 1908 * '''Yagokoro-omoikane Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Hata Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Ichigami Shrine (Horinwa Gongen)''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Matsuo Shrine''' * '''Kotohira Shrine''' * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' ==Nearby Sites== * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu-ga-oka Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} ==References== * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 (Kiryu City History) |year=1958 |publisher=桐生市史刊行委員会 |editor=桐生市史編纂委員会}} * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 別巻 (Kiryu City History - Supplement) |year=1971 |publisher=桐生市役所 |editor=桐生市史別巻編集委員会}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/ Official Kiryu City Website] ==See Also== * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|美和神社 (桐生市)|version=101958614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) [[File:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall == Overview == '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is located in {{ill|Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|ja|宮本町 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|1=Miyamoto-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454887}}, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Ranked as a "small shrine," and was previously designated as a Gōsha (local shrine) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine venerates the deities [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (Ōmononushi Kushi Mikatama no Mikoto) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takesu Susanoo no Mikoto). == History == The shrine is traditionally believed to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Sujin, the 10th emperor of Japan. It is said to have been dedicated to the deities brought from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. In Enryaku 15 (796), the shrine was Ranked as an official shrine along with {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|ja|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634952}} (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]). During the Gangyō period (880), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Junior Grade, alongside other local shrines, as documented in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Sandai Jitsuroku]]. According to the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine was listed as "Miwa Daimyōjin of First Rank." Unfortunately, during the Tenshō era, a fire destroyed many of the shrine's old records and treasures. Historically, the temple {{ill|Enmanji (Kiryu)|ja|円満寺 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmanji (Kiryu)|1=Enman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394812}} served as its bettō-ji (attached temple). During the Edo period, the local Kojima family managed both the shrine and its associated temple. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was officially designated as a Gōsha (local shrine). In 1908, the [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Kiryu)]] of Honmachi 3-chome, previously dedicated to Gozu Tenno and attached to Shujōin Temple, was merged into Miwa Shrine. == Sub-Shrines (境内社) == * Yasaka Shrine – Previously a village shrine, merged in 1908. * Yaokotonashi Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Kikami Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Ichigami Shrine (Hōrinkan Gongen) – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Matsuo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} == Cultural Events == The shrine is the center of the {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}}, held annually from November 19 to 20. It features Kagura dances and taiko drum performances. Additionally, the shrine is involved in the {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}}, where its mikoshi (portable shrine) is stored in an adjacent warehouse. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Kiryu Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Park|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|Kiryu City North Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu City North Elementary School|1=Kiryu City North Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Kiryu)}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|美和神社 (桐生市)|version=101958614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Ranked as a "small shrine," and was previously designated as a Gōsha (local shrine) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine venerates the deities [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (Ōmononushi Kushi Mikatama no Mikoto) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takesu Susanoo no Mikoto). == History == The shrine is traditionally believed to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Sujin, the 10th emperor of Japan. It is said to have been dedicated to the deities brought from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. In Enryaku 15 (796), the shrine was Ranked as an official shrine along with {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|ja|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634952}} (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]). During the Gangyō period (880), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Junior Grade, alongside other local shrines, as documented in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Sandai Jitsuroku]]. According to the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine was listed as "Miwa Daimyōjin of First Rank." Unfortunately, during the Tenshō era, a fire destroyed many of the shrine's old records and treasures. Historically, the temple {{ill|Enmanji (Kiryu)|ja|円満寺 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmanji (Kiryu)|1=Enman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394812}} served as its bettō-ji (attached temple). During the Edo period, the local Kojima family managed both the shrine and its associated temple. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was officially designated as a Gōsha (local shrine). In 1908, the [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Kiryu)]] of Honmachi 3-chome, previously dedicated to Gozu Tenno and attached to Shujōin Temple, was merged into Miwa Shrine. == Sub-Shrines (境内社) == * Yasaka Shrine – Previously a village shrine, merged in 1908. * Yaokotonashi Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Kikami Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Ichigami Shrine (Hōrinkan Gongen) – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Matsuo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} == Cultural Events == The shrine is the center of the {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}}, held annually from November 19 to 20. It features Kagura dances and taiko drum performances. Additionally, the shrine is involved in the {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}}, where its mikoshi (portable shrine) is stored in an adjacent warehouse. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Kiryu Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Park|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|Kiryu City North Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu City North Elementary School|1=Kiryu City North Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to [[sn:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu)]][[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608848]] Wikidata name: Miwa-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miwa Shrine | native_name = 美和神社 | image = File:Miwa Shrine of Kiryu.JPG | caption = Worship Hall (Haiden) | map_type = Japan Gunma | map_caption = location in Gunma Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.417833|N|139.33975|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-1-1 Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | established = During the reign of Emperor Sujin | former_Rank = {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha (minor)|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft（小）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha (郷社)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | festivals = November 19–20 (Kiryu Ebisu Festival) | prefecture= {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社, *Miwa Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is listed as a minor {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. ==Overview== Miwa Shrine enshrines {{ill|2=ja|Ōmononushi Kushi-mika-tama-no-Mikoto|ja|大物主櫛甕玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} and {{ill|2=ja|Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|武速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. It is situated at the southeastern foot of Mount Agatsuma in Kiryu, facing Yamate-dori Avenue. The shrine is known for hosting performances of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] and taiko drumming during the annual {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} held from November 19–20. A mikoshi (portable shrine) storehouse for the {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} is located adjacent to the worship hall. ==History== Miwa Shrine is traditionally said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin, with its deity transferred from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. It was officially recognized as a shrine in 796 (Enryaku 15) alongside [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In 880 (Gangyō 4), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, alongside other shrines such as [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]], {{ill|2=ja|Kohachisu Shrine|ja|甲波宿禰社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and {{ill|2=ja|Oiwai Shrine|ja|小祝社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The shrine was referred to as "Conferred First Rank Miwa Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|2=ja|Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. Unfortunately, it lost many of its historical records and treasures in a fire during the Tenshō era. Miwa Shrine was originally affiliated with {{ill|2=ja|Enman-ji Temple|ja|円満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and both were managed by the Kojima family during the Edo period. It was designated as a Gōsha in 1872 (Meiji 5) and merged with the former [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] in 1908 (Meiji 41). ==Sub-Shrines== * '''Yasaka Shrine''' – Former Village shrine, merged in 1908 * '''Yagokoro-omoikane Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Hata Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Ichigami Shrine (Horinwa Gongen)''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Matsuo Shrine''' * '''Kotohira Shrine''' * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' ==Nearby Sites== * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu-ga-oka Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} ==References== * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 (Kiryu City History) |year=1958 |publisher=桐生市史刊行委員会 |editor=桐生市史編纂委員会}} * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 別巻 (Kiryu City History - Supplement) |year=1971 |publisher=桐生市役所 |editor=桐生市史別巻編集委員会}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/ Official Kiryu City Website] ==See Also== * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|美和神社 (桐生市)|version=101958614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) [[File:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall == Overview == '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is located in {{ill|Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|ja|宮本町 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|1=Miyamoto-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454887}}, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Ranked as a "small shrine," and was previously designated as a Gōsha (local shrine) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine venerates the deities [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (Ōmononushi Kushi Mikatama no Mikoto) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takesu Susanoo no Mikoto). == History == The shrine is traditionally believed to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Sujin, the 10th emperor of Japan. It is said to have been dedicated to the deities brought from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. In Enryaku 15 (796), the shrine was Ranked as an official shrine along with {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|ja|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634952}} (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]). During the Gangyō period (880), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Junior Grade, alongside other local shrines, as documented in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Sandai Jitsuroku]]. According to the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine was listed as "Miwa Daimyōjin of First Rank." Unfortunately, during the Tenshō era, a fire destroyed many of the shrine's old records and treasures. Historically, the temple {{ill|Enmanji (Kiryu)|ja|円満寺 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmanji (Kiryu)|1=Enman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394812}} served as its bettō-ji (attached temple). During the Edo period, the local Kojima family managed both the shrine and its associated temple. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was officially designated as a Gōsha (local shrine). In 1908, the [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Kiryu)]] of Honmachi 3-chome, previously dedicated to Gozu Tenno and attached to Shujōin Temple, was merged into Miwa Shrine. == Sub-Shrines (境内社) == * Yasaka Shrine – Previously a village shrine, merged in 1908. * Yaokotonashi Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Kikami Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Ichigami Shrine (Hōrinkan Gongen) – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Matsuo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} == Cultural Events == The shrine is the center of the {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}}, held annually from November 19 to 20. It features Kagura dances and taiko drum performances. Additionally, the shrine is involved in the {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}}, where its mikoshi (portable shrine) is stored in an adjacent warehouse. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Kiryu Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu [[sn:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu)]][[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608848]] Wikidata name: Miwa-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miwa Shrine | native_name = 美和神社 | image = File:Miwa Shrine of Kiryu.JPG | caption = Worship Hall (Haiden) | map_type = Japan Gunma | map_caption = location in Gunma Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.417833|N|139.33975|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-1-1 Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | established = During the reign of Emperor Sujin | former_Rank = {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha (minor)|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft（小）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha (郷社)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | festivals = November 19–20 (Kiryu Ebisu Festival) | prefecture= {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社, *Miwa Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Miyamoto-chō, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is listed as a minor {{ill|2=ja|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|2=ja|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. ==Overview== Miwa Shrine enshrines {{ill|2=ja|Ōmononushi Kushi-mika-tama-no-Mikoto|ja|大物主櫛甕玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} and {{ill|2=ja|Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|武速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. It is situated at the southeastern foot of Mount Agatsuma in Kiryu, facing Yamate-dori Avenue. The shrine is known for hosting performances of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] and taiko drumming during the annual {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} held from November 19–20. A mikoshi (portable shrine) storehouse for the {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} is located adjacent to the worship hall. ==History== Miwa Shrine is traditionally said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin, with its deity transferred from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. It was officially recognized as a shrine in 796 (Enryaku 15) alongside [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In 880 (Gangyō 4), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, alongside other shrines such as [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]], {{ill|2=ja|Kohachisu Shrine|ja|甲波宿禰社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and {{ill|2=ja|Oiwai Shrine|ja|小祝社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The shrine was referred to as "Conferred First Rank Miwa Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|2=ja|Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. Unfortunately, it lost many of its historical records and treasures in a fire during the Tenshō era. Miwa Shrine was originally affiliated with {{ill|2=ja|Enman-ji Temple|ja|円満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and both were managed by the Kojima family during the Edo period. It was designated as a Gōsha in 1872 (Meiji 5) and merged with the former [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] in 1908 (Meiji 41). ==Sub-Shrines== * '''Yasaka Shrine''' – Former Village shrine, merged in 1908 * '''Yagokoro-omoikane Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Hata Shrine''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Ichigami Shrine (Horinwa Gongen)''' – Former precinct shrine of Yasaka Shrine * '''Matsuo Shrine''' * '''Kotohira Shrine''' * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' ==Nearby Sites== * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu-ga-oka Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|2=ja|Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA|1=Kiryu City Kita Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} ==References== * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 (Kiryu City History) |year=1958 |publisher=桐生市史刊行委員会 |editor=桐生市史編纂委員会}} * {{Cite book |title=桐生市史 別巻 (Kiryu City History - Supplement) |year=1971 |publisher=桐生市役所 |editor=桐生市史別巻編集委員会}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/ Official Kiryu City Website] ==See Also== * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|美和神社 (桐生市)|version=101958614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:美和神社 (桐生市)]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) [[File:Miwa-jinja (Kiryu), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall == Overview == '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is located in {{ill|Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|ja|宮本町 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamoto Town, Kiryu|1=Miyamoto-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454887}}, Kiryu City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Ranked as a "small shrine," and was previously designated as a Gōsha (local shrine) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine venerates the deities [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (Ōmononushi Kushi Mikatama no Mikoto) and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takesu Susanoo no Mikoto). == History == The shrine is traditionally believed to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Sujin, the 10th emperor of Japan. It is said to have been dedicated to the deities brought from Mount Miwa in Yamato Province. In Enryaku 15 (796), the shrine was Ranked as an official shrine along with {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|ja|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634952}} (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]). During the Gangyō period (880), it was awarded the Rank of Senior Fifth Rank, Junior Grade, alongside other local shrines, as documented in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Sandai Jitsuroku]]. According to the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine was listed as "Miwa Daimyōjin of First Rank." Unfortunately, during the Tenshō era, a fire destroyed many of the shrine's old records and treasures. Historically, the temple {{ill|Enmanji (Kiryu)|ja|円満寺 (桐生市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmanji (Kiryu)|1=Enman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394812}} served as its bettō-ji (attached temple). During the Edo period, the local Kojima family managed both the shrine and its associated temple. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was officially designated as a Gōsha (local shrine). In 1908, the [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Kiryu)]] of Honmachi 3-chome, previously dedicated to Gozu Tenno and attached to Shujōin Temple, was merged into Miwa Shrine. == Sub-Shrines (境内社) == * Yasaka Shrine – Previously a village shrine, merged in 1908. * Yaokotonashi Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Kikami Shrine – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Ichigami Shrine (Hōrinkan Gongen) – Former sub-shrine of Yasaka Shrine. * Matsuo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} == Cultural Events == The shrine is the center of the {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}}, held annually from November 19 to 20. It features Kagura dances and taiko drum performances. Additionally, the shrine is involved in the {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}}, where its mikoshi (portable shrine) is stored in an adjacent warehouse. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Kiryu Park|ja|桐生が岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Park|1=Kiryū-ga-oka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537258}} * {{ill|Kiryu City North Elementary School|ja|桐生市立北小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu City North Elementary School|1=Kiryu City North Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537291}} == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|桐生西宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja|桐生ゑびす講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Ebisu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 桐生西宮神社|1=Kiryū Nishinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537354}} * {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|ja|桐生八木節まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|1=Kiryū Yagibushi festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537271}} == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Kiryu)}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated
